eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: SpriteMultitouchJoystick
description: Multitouch joysticks are objects showing a joystick on the screen, useful for mobile. They work like a physical gamepad:

- Automatic "mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the virtual joystick (it works by reading the multitouch joystick state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.
There are ready-to-use joysticks in the asset-store [multitouch joysticks pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=multitouch-joysticks-multitouch-joysticks).

events.comment: Move thumb back to center when not being pressed (acts like a spring on a real controller)

parameters: AngleBetweenPositions(Object.CenterX(), Object.CenterY(), TouchX(TouchId, Object.Layer(), 0), TouchY(TouchId, Object.Layer(), 0))
AngleBetweenPositions(Object.CenterX(), Object.CenterY(), TouchX(TouchId, Object.Layer(), 0), TouchY(TouchId, Object.Layer(), 0))
clamp(2 * DistanceBetweenPositions(Object.CenterX(), Object.CenterY(), TouchX(TouchId, Object.Layer(), 0), TouchY(TouchId, Object.Layer(), 0)) / Object.Width(), 0, 1)

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: FirstPersonCamera
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Move the camera to look though objects eyes.
description: Move the camera to look though objects eyes.
This extension can be used with the "Mouse pointer lock" or "Gamepad" extension to build a first person camera.
The first person example uses this extension ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://3d-first-person)).

eventsFunctions.description: Move the camera to look though the object eyes. The object must look to the right when all its angles are 0 and the top of its head be toward Z+.
eventsFunctions.name: LookFromObjectEyes
eventsFunctions.sentence: Move the camera of _PARAM2_ to look though _PARAM1_ eyes

eventsFunctions.description: Move the camera to look though the object eyes. The object must look to the right when all its angles are 0 and the top of its head be toward Z+.
eventsFunctions.name: LookFrom3DObjectEyes
eventsFunctions.sentence: Move the camera of _PARAM3_ to look though _PARAM1_ eyes

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: @Lizard-13
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: SnapToGrid
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Snap objects on a virtual grid.
description: It allows to snap objects on a *virtual 2D grid*.
A [simple example](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://snap-object-to-grid) shows how to snap objects that are dragged with the mouse.

eventsFunctions.description: Snap object to a virtual grid (i.e: this is not the grid used in the editor).
eventsFunctions.fullName: Snap objects to a virtual grid
eventsFunctions.name: SnapObjectToVirtualGrid
eventsFunctions.sentence: Snap _PARAM1_ to a virtual grid using cells with width: _PARAM2_px, height _PARAM3_px and an offset position (_PARAM4_; _PARAM5_)

events.comment: Round the Object position to snap to the in-game grid

parameters.description: Objects to snap to the virtual grid
parameters.name: Object

parameters.description: Width of a cell of the virtual grid (in pixels)
parameters.name: CellWidth

parameters.description: Height of a cell of the virtual grid (in pixels)
parameters.name: CellHeight

parameters.description: Offset on the X axis of the virtual grid (in pixels)
parameters.name: OffsetX

parameters.description: Offset on the Y axis of the virtual grid (in pixels)
parameters.name: OffsetY
